context: This chapter describes the various types of charitable gifts (dana) and their spiritual merits. It covers the proper recipients, times, and modes of giving, including cow donation, food donation, gold, land, water, and other gifts that bring merit to the giver both in this life and the next.

verses.hindi:
गरुड उवाच —
दानानां च विधिं ब्रूहि पुण्यं यत् परमं भवेत् ।
केन दानेन को लोको लभ्यते पुरुषोत्तम ॥
verses.transliteration:
Garuḍa uvāca —
dānānāṃ ca vidhiṃ brūhi puṇyaṃ yat paramaṃ bhavet |
kena dānena ko loko labhyate Puruṣottama ||
verses.translation: Garuda said: Tell me the rules of gifts and the supreme merit thereof. By which gift is which world obtained, O Supreme Lord?

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
श्रृणु तार्क्ष्य प्रवक्ष्यामि दानधर्मं सनातनम् ।
येन दत्तेन मनुजः सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvāca —
śṛṇu Tārkṣya pravakṣyāmi dāna-dharmaṃ sanātanam |
yena dattena manujaḥ sarva-pāpaiḥ pramucyate ||
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: Listen, O Tarksya, I shall describe the eternal law of giving. By such giving a man is released from all sins.

verses.hindi:
सर्वेषामेव दानानामन्नदानं विशिष्यते ।
अन्नादेव हि जीवन्ति प्राणिनः सचराचराः ॥
verses.transliteration:
sarveṣām eva dānānām anna-dānaṃ viśiṣyate |
annād eva hi jīvanti prāṇinaḥ sa-carācarāḥ ||
verses.translation: Of all gifts, the gift of food is the most excellent. All living beings, moving and unmoving, live by food alone.

verses.hindi:
अन्नदानं प्रशंसन्ति मुनयः सत्यवादिनः ।
अन्नदानात् परं दानं न भूतं न भविष्यति ॥
verses.transliteration:
anna-dānaṃ praśaṃsanti munayaḥ satya-vādinaḥ |
anna-dānāt paraṃ dānaṃ na bhūtaṃ na bhaviṣyati ||
verses.translation: The truthful sages praise the gift of food. Greater than the gift of food there has never been nor will there ever be a gift.

verses.hindi:
अन्नदानेन तृप्तस्य प्राणिनो यत् सुखं भवेत् ।
तत् सुखं लभते दाता परलोके न संशयः ॥
verses.transliteration:
anna-dānena tṛptasya prāṇino yat sukhaṃ bhavet |
tat sukhaṃ labhate dātā paraloke na saṃśayaḥ ||
verses.translation: Whatever happiness a creature obtains from being satisfied by the gift of food, that same happiness the giver obtains in the next world — there is no doubt.

verses.hindi:
गोदानं परमं दानं गावो लोकस्य मातरः ।
गावः सर्वसुखप्रदाः पुण्या धन्याश्च गावः स्युः ॥
verses.transliteration:
go-dānaṃ paramaṃ dānaṃ gāvo lokasya mātaraḥ |
gāvaḥ sarva-sukha-pradāḥ puṇyā dhanyāś ca gāvaḥ syuḥ ||
verses.translation: The gift of a cow is the supreme gift. Cows are the mothers of the world. Cows bestow all happiness; they are holy and blessed.

verses.hindi:
गवां मध्ये स्थितो विष्णुर्गावो विष्णुपदे स्थिताः ।
गवां यत्रैव तिष्ठन्ति तत्र तीर्थं न संशयः ॥
verses.transliteration:
gavāṃ madhye sthito Viṣṇur gāvo Viṣṇu-pade sthitāḥ |
gavāṃ yatraiva tiṣṭhanti tatra tīrthaṃ na saṃśayaḥ ||
verses.translation: Vishnu resides amidst the cows, and cows dwell in the abode of Vishnu. Wherever cows reside, there is a holy place — there is no doubt.

verses.hindi:
एकां गां विप्रमुख्याय यो ददाति समाहितः ।
स तारयेत् सप्तकुलं प्रेत्यभावे न सीदति ॥
verses.transliteration:
ekāṃ gāṃ vipra-mukhyāya yo dadāti samāhitaḥ |
sa tārayet sapta-kulaṃ pretyabhāve na sīdati ||
verses.translation: He who, with concentration, gives one cow to an eminent Brahmana, saves seven generations and does not suffer in the afterlife.

verses.hindi:
भूमिदानं महादानं भूमिदानात् परं न हि ।
भूमिं यो ब्राह्मणे दद्याद् विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
bhūmi-dānaṃ mahā-dānaṃ bhūmi-dānāt paraṃ na hi |
bhūmiṃ yo brāhmaṇe dadyād Viṣṇu-lokaṃ sa gacchati ||
verses.translation: The gift of land is a great gift; there is none higher than the gift of land. He who gives land to a Brahmana goes to the world of Vishnu.

verses.hindi:
सुवर्णदानं परमं सुवर्णं ज्योतिरुच्यते ।
अग्निवर्णं महातेजो दत्त्वा स्वर्गे महीयते ॥
verses.transliteration:
suvarṇa-dānaṃ paramaṃ suvarṇaṃ jyotir ucyate |
agni-varṇaṃ mahā-tejo dattvā svarge mahīyate ||
verses.translation: The gift of gold is supreme. Gold is called radiance — fire-colored and greatly lustrous. Having given it, one is glorified in heaven.

verses.hindi:
तिलदानं महादानं कृष्णवर्णं विशेषतः ।
तिला यमप्रिया ह्येते तिलदानं प्रशस्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
tila-dānaṃ mahā-dānaṃ kṛṣṇa-varṇaṃ viśeṣataḥ |
tilā yama-priyā hy ete tila-dānaṃ praśasyate ||
verses.translation: The gift of sesame is a great gift, especially black sesame. Sesame seeds are dear to Yama; therefore the gift of sesame is highly praised.

verses.hindi:
वस्त्रदानं प्रशंसन्ति सर्वे धर्मविदो जनाः ।
वस्त्रदानात् परं पुण्यं वस्त्रहीनो न मानवः ॥
verses.transliteration:
vastra-dānaṃ praśaṃsanti sarve dharma-vido janāḥ |
vastra-dānāt paraṃ puṇyaṃ vastra-hīno na mānavaḥ ||
verses.translation: All people versed in dharma praise the gift of clothing. Great merit comes from the gift of clothing; without clothing a man is not truly human.

verses.hindi:
जलदानं महापुण्यं निर्जले प्रान्तरे वने ।
तृषार्तेभ्यो जलं दद्याद् अक्षयं तस्य तत् फलम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
jala-dānaṃ mahā-puṇyaṃ nirjale prāntare vane |
tṛṣārtebhyo jalaṃ dadyād akṣayaṃ tasya tat phalam ||
verses.translation: The gift of water is of great merit, especially in waterless regions and forests. One who gives water to the thirsty obtains inexhaustible fruit.

verses.hindi:
कूपं वापीं तडागं च यः करोति नरोत्तमः ।
दशपूर्वान् दशापरान् आत्मानं चैव तारयेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
kūpaṃ vāpīṃ taḍāgaṃ ca yaḥ karoti narottamaḥ |
daśa-pūrvān daśāparān ātmānaṃ caiva tārayet ||
verses.translation: The best of men who constructs a well, a step-well, or a tank saves ten generations before him, ten after him, and himself.

verses.hindi:
दीपदानं प्रशस्तं स्याज्ज्ञानदीपं विशेषतः ।
तमोनाशकरं दीपं दत्त्वा ज्योतिर्मयो भवेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
dīpa-dānaṃ praśastaṃ syāj jñāna-dīpaṃ viśeṣataḥ |
tamo-nāśa-karaṃ dīpaṃ dattvā jyotirmayo bhavet ||
verses.translation: The gift of a lamp is praiseworthy, especially the lamp of knowledge. Having given a lamp that destroys darkness, one becomes full of light.

verses.hindi:
विद्यादानं महादानं सर्वदानेषु चोत्तमम् ।
विद्यादानेन विप्राय ब्रह्मलोकं स गच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
vidyā-dānaṃ mahā-dānaṃ sarva-dāneṣu cottamam |
vidyā-dānena viprāya brahma-lokaṃ sa gacchati ||
verses.translation: The gift of knowledge is a great gift, the highest among all gifts. By giving knowledge to a Brahmana, one goes to the world of Brahma.

verses.hindi:
अभयं सर्वभूतेभ्यो यो ददाति दयापरः ।
तस्य देहं विनिर्मुक्तं न भयं विद्यते क्वचित् ॥
verses.transliteration:
abhayaṃ sarva-bhūtebhyo yo dadāti dayāparaḥ |
tasya dehaṃ vinirmuktaṃ na bhayaṃ vidyate kvacit ||
verses.translation: He who, being compassionate, gives fearlessness to all beings — after leaving his body, he finds no fear anywhere.

verses.hindi:
श्रद्धया परया दानं सत्पात्रे विनियोजितम् ।
अनन्तफलदं ज्ञेयं तस्मात् श्रद्धां समाचरेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
śraddhayā parayā dānaṃ sat-pātre viniyojitam |
ananta-phala-daṃ jñeyaṃ tasmāt śraddhāṃ samācaret ||
verses.translation: A gift given with supreme faith to a worthy recipient should be known as yielding infinite fruit. Therefore one should practice giving with faith.

verses.hindi:
अश्रद्धया तु यद् दत्तं तत् कालान्तरनाशकम् ।
तस्माच्छ्रद्धापूर्वकं दानं कर्तव्यं मुक्तिमिच्छता ॥
verses.transliteration:
aśraddhayā tu yad dattaṃ tat kālāntara-nāśakam |
tasmāc chraddhā-pūrvakaṃ dānaṃ kartavyaṃ muktim icchatā ||
verses.translation: That which is given without faith perishes with time. Therefore, one who desires liberation should make gifts preceded by faith.

verses.hindi:
उत्तमो ब्राह्मणः पात्रं दानं तस्मै विशिष्यते ।
वेदविद् ब्रह्मनिष्ठश्च शीलवान् दम उच्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
uttamo brāhmaṇaḥ pātraṃ dānaṃ tasmai viśiṣyate |
veda-vid brahma-niṣṭhaś ca śīlavān dama ucyate ||
verses.translation: The best recipient is a Brahmana; a gift to him is most distinguished. He should be a knower of the Vedas, devoted to Brahman, virtuous, and self-controlled.

verses.hindi:
पर्वकाले च दानं यत् संक्रान्तौ विशेषतः ।
ग्रहणे चन्द्रसूर्ययोर्विशेषफलदं भवेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
parva-kāle ca dānaṃ yat saṃkrāntau viśeṣataḥ |
grahaṇe candra-sūryayor viśeṣa-phala-daṃ bhavet ||
verses.translation: Gifts given at festival times, especially at solstices, and during eclipses of the moon and sun, yield special fruit.

verses.hindi:
एतानि दानधर्माणि यः पठेत् श्रृणुयात् तथा ।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
etāni dāna-dharmāṇi yaḥ paṭhet śṛṇuyāt tathā |
sarva-pāpa-vinirmukto Viṣṇu-lokaṃ sa gacchati ||
verses.translation: He who reads or hears these laws of giving, freed from all sins, goes to the world of Vishnu.
